extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE attacked with 200m remaining on 12% Mas de la Costa after QUINTANA set pace for him, dropped ROGLIC/LOPEZ by 6 seconds
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma used KUSS then BENNETT sequential pacing to isolate ROGLIC, indicating deep climbing domestique strength even on brutal gradients.
QUINTANA paced for VALVERDE on steepest section despite being quoted as saying Valverde was sole leader, revealing Movistar's actual hierarchy.
The final 12%+ gradient selected down to precisely 4 riders midway up, showing that super-steep finishes create earlier selection than moderate gradients.
Movistar set tempo from penultimate climb through base of final wall, suggesting they preferred attrition over late attacks against Roglic.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: at 39 demonstrated superior acceleration on 12%+ gradient with 200m to go, confirming he peaks on super-steep pitches
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: worked for Valverde despite comparable form, losing 6 seconds while domestiquing reveals team tactics cost him GC position
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: matched top-3 until final 200m but couldn't respond to Valverde's kick on 18% maximum sections
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: set early tempo on 12% climb putting most GC riders in trouble, confirming pure climbing strength at altitude
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: negative: finished 20+ seconds behind top-4 with Majka group, suggesting not yet at Grand Tour GC contention level in 2019
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: negative: dropped on penultimate climb despite holding race lead, revealing one-day climber limitations on consecutive MTF stages
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: positive: limited losses and fought back to finish with Pogacar group after two difficult years, indicating form recovery trajectory
